children.label: A
children.text: the members of which are appointed by all of the school boards of the schools located within an agency, including schools operated under contract or grant; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of such members is determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the affected tribes;

children.heading: Bureau
children.label: 2 Bureau
children.text: The term “Bureau” means the Bureau of Indian Affairs of the Department of the Interior.

children.label: A
children.text: a Bureau school;

children.label: B
children.text: a contract or grant school; or

children.label: C
children.text: a school for which assistance is provided under the Tribally Controlled Schools Act of 1988 [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 2501

children.heading: Bureau school
children.label: 4 Bureau school
children.text: The term “Bureau school” means a Bureau-operated elementary or secondary day or boarding school or a Bureau-operated dormitory for students attending a school other than a Bureau school.

children.heading: Complementary educational facilities
children.label: 5 Complementary educational facilities
children.text: The term “complementary educational facilities” means educational program functional spaces such as libraries, gymnasiums, and cafeterias.

children.heading: Contract or grant school
children.label: 6 Contract or grant school
children.text: The term “contract or grant school” means an elementary school, secondary school, or dormitory that receives financial assistance for its operation under a contract, grant, or agreement with the Bureau under section 5321, 5322(a), or 5352 of this title, or under the Tribally Controlled Schools Act of 1988 [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 2501

children.heading: Director
children.label: 7 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Office of Indian Education Programs.

children.heading: Education line officer
children.label: 8 Education line officer
children.text: The term “education line officer” means a member of the education personnel under the supervision of the Director of the Office, whether located in a central, area, or agency office.

children.heading: Family literacy services
children.label: 9 Family literacy services
children.text: The term “family literacy services” has the meaning given that term in .section 7801 of title 20

children.heading: Financial plan
children.label: 10 Financial plan
children.text: The term “financial plan” means a plan of services provided by each Bureau school.

children.heading: Indian organization
children.label: 11 Indian organization
children.text: The term “Indian organization” means any group, association, partnership, corporation, or other legal entity owned or controlled by a federally recognized Indian tribe or tribes, or a majority of whose members are members of federally recognized tribes.

children.label: A
children.text: the allocation and obligation of Federal funds and determinations as to the amounts of expenditures;

children.label: B
children.text: the administration of Federal personnel laws for Federal employees;

children.label: C
children.text: the administration of Federal contracting and grant laws, including the monitoring and auditing of contracts and grants in order to maintain the continuing trust, programmatic, and fiscal responsibilities of the Secretary;

children.label: D
children.text: the conducting of administrative hearings and deciding of administrative appeals;

children.label: E
children.text: the determination of the Secretary’s views and recommendations concerning administrative appeals or litigation and the representation of the Secretary in administrative appeals and litigation;

children.label: F
children.text: the issuance of Federal regulations and policies as well as any documents published in the Federal Register;

children.label: G
children.text: reporting to Congress and the President;

children.label: H
children.text: the formulation of the Secretary’s and the President’s policies and their budgetary and legislative recommendations and views; and

children.label: I
children.text: the nondelegable statutory duties of the Secretary relating to trust resources.

children.heading: Local educational agency
children.label: 13 Local educational agency
children.text: The term “local educational agency” means a board of education or other legally constituted local school authority having administrative control and direction of free public education in a county, township, or independent or other school district located within a State, and includes any State agency that directly operates and maintains facilities for providing free public education.

children.label: A
children.text: the members of the body shall be appointed by the tribal governing bodies of the tribes affected; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of such members shall be determined by the Secretary in consultation with the affected tribes.

children.heading: Office
children.label: 15 Office
children.text: The term “Office” means the Office of Indian Education Programs within the Bureau.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “regulation” means any part of a statement of general or particular applicability of the Secretary designed to carry out, interpret, or prescribe law or policy in carrying out this Act.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: B Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) or any other provision of this chapter shall be construed to prohibit the Secretary from issuing guidance, internal directives, or other documents similar to the documents found in the Indian Affairs Manual of the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 17 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Supervisor
children.label: 18 Supervisor
children.text: The term “supervisor” means the individual in the position of ultimate authority at a Bureau school.

children.heading: Tribal governing body
children.label: 19 Tribal governing body
children.text: The term “tribal governing body” means, with respect to any school, the tribal governing body, or tribal governing bodies, that represent at least 90 percent of the students served by such school.

children.heading: Tribe
children.label: 20 Tribe
children.text: The term “tribe” means any Indian tribe, band, nation, or other organized group or community, including an Alaska Native Regional Corporation or Village Corporation (as defined in or established pursuant to the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act [ et seq.]), which is recognized as eligible for the special programs and services provided by the United States to Indians because of their status as Indians.43 U.S.C. 1601
